mnemonics.title: Compound Operators Remember
mnemonics.content:
ARITHMETIC: a OP= b → a = a OP b
Add SUBtract MULTIPLY DIVide MODulo
+ - * / %

Remember: Put the OP before =

acronyms.usage: a = b = c = value assigns right to left

visualTips.visualization: Think of '=' as an arrow pointing RIGHT. Value flows from right to left: x = 5 means value 5 flows INTO x
a += b is like a bubble that吸入 (吸入) the result of a+b back into a itself

analogies.analogy: Like putting something in a box. The box (variable) gets the value inside it.
Like giving two people the same house address. They both point to the same house, not having their own copies.
Like saying 'add 5 to my score' instead of 'my score = my score + 5'
